assumptions: No AssemblyAI account, API key, or transcript is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; jq is helpful for the documented poll example. No language SDK is assumed.

developer_goal: Create an AssemblyAI account and API key, then transcribe the hosted sample over HTTPS without installing an SDK.

first_success.outcome: Authenticated cURL submit and poll against https://api.assemblyai.com/v2/transcript return completed transcript text for the hosted wildfires sample.
first_success.observable_signal: GET /v2/transcript/{id} returns status completed and text beginning with the wildfires sample content.

route_selection.surface: Pre-recorded transcription via cURL HTTP API (API key + free trial credits)
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: Pre-recorded transcription via cURL HTTP API (API key + free trial credits)

candidates.name: Pre-recorded transcription via cURL HTTP API (API key + free trial credits)
candidates.reason: The AssemblyAI Quickstart documents Using the HTTP API directly with cURL: authorization header (no Bearer prefix), POST /v2/transcript, then poll GET /v2/transcript/{id} until completed. Support docs state a limited free trial includes pre-recorded transcription with $50 of audio processing. An SDK install is not required.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to an AssemblyAI account.
Confirm free-trial credits remain for pre-recorded transcription (or add a card and funds if the free $50 limit is exhausted).
required_path.observable_result: Billing shows remaining free-trial balance or positive funded balance.

required_path.action: Create or copy an API key from the dashboard and store it securely.
required_path.observable_result: The key value is available locally.
The environment variable is set for the current shell.

required_path.action: Submit a transcript with cURL POST to https://api.assemblyai.com/v2/transcript, then poll GET until status is completed.
required_path.observable_result: status is completed and text is present.

required_path.action: Confirm the transcript text is printed from the completed JSON.
required_path.observable_result: Transcript text for the wildfires sample is visible.

prerequisites.description: An AssemblyAI account that can open the dashboard API Keys page.
Usable free-trial audio processing credits (up to $50). Adding a credit card upgrades after the free limit; it is not required for the first free-trial call.
An API key sent in the authorization header (no Bearer prefix).
A terminal with cURL (and jq for the documented poll loop). No language SDK is required.

excluded.item: Speaker labels and language detection options
Paid top-up beyond free-trial first success

sources.title: Quickstart - Transcribe a pre-recorded audio file
Can I sign up for free?
evidence_supported: Credit card upgrade / top-up after limit

uncertainties.question: Exact email/Google signup field sequence after the first provider choice.
uncertainties.impact: Account-step field inventory stays coarse for demos.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Public signup shell does not enumerate every downstream registration state.
